ground.name: ground
ground.description: This radical consists of a single, horizontal stroke. What's the biggest, single, horizontal stroke? That's the ground. Look at the ground, look at this radical, now look at the ground again. Kind of the same, right?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

two.name: two
two.description: One line plus another line equals two lines. This radical represents the number two. Pretty simple, right? Can you guess what three looks like? I bet you can...

nine.name: nine
nine.description: If you look at this radical in just the right way, you'll notice that it kind of looks like the letters "n" and "i" (the "i" comes from the little flippy-up stroke on the right side). What do the letters "n" and "i" start spelling? Why, the word nine.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

seven.name: seven
seven.description: What does this radical look like? Hmm, nothing much. What about if you turn it upside down? Now does it look like a 7 with a line through it? Close enough to help you to remember that this radical is seven, right? Just remember, when you see this radical, flip it over and see what number it looks like. If you can do that, you can remember the meaning!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

person.name: person
person.description: This is a person. They're standing with their arms down at their sides and their legs spread out, which kind of makes them look like a torso with a pair of legs. Think of 人 as highlighting the bipedal nature of people. Walking on two legs? Probably a person.

enter.name: enter
enter.description: Watch out! This isn't the person radical, it's something else. What's the difference? The right side is longer than the left. This radical also has that extra part sticking out at the top. See it? It kind of looks like a person lowering their head to go inside a tent or something. That's why this radical is enter.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fins.name: fins
fins.description: These look like fins. What's a pair of disembodied fins doing here?! Some fish must have left them here by accident. Bet they're really missing these fins right about now.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

power.name: power
power.description: Imagine a guy flexing his arm downward to show off his steroid-pumped arm. It's huge. It's so full of power. RARRRWWWRRR!!

cross.name: cross
cross.description: This radical looks like a cross. I mean, just look at it. Two bars going through each other = a cross!

toe.name: toe
toe.description: This radical kind of looks like a toe. See the one weird toe coming off the side of it? This also looks like the katakana character ト (to), which sounds like the word "toe." So whenever you see ト, just think toe.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

mouth.name: mouth
mouth.description: A big, gaping, square hole represents someone opening their giant mouth to tell you something stupid.

big.name: big
big.description: This radical looks just like a stick figure—more specifically, a really big guy with his arms stretched out nice and big. When you see this radical, just think big, because this guy is really BIG!

woman.name: woman
woman.description: This radical looks like a woman curtsying. Another way to think of it could be as two stacked Xs, since women generally have XX chromosomes.

mountain.name: mountain
mountain.description: This is a mountain. See the big peak in the middle and the smaller ones on either side? This totally looks like a mountain.

river.name: river
river.description: This radical already looks like a river. See how the lines represent the flow of the river? There's even a slight bend in this river. So when you see this radical, you should think "river."

construction.name: construction
construction.description: You know those big steel girders that are used for the frame of big buildings? This is like the end of one of those, and has a very distinct shape, just like this radical. These girders are used in construction, which is why this radical means construction. Funny thing is, you use these girders to put together buildings just like you're going to use this radical to put together kanji!

sword.name: sword
sword.description: This is the sword radical. The ノ part is the curvy sword, while the rest is a samurai bowing deeply, like 90 degrees, as he holds that sword — probably before a sword fight. By the way, don't confuse this with the power radical (力). See the difference? In the sword radical (刀), the ノ doesn't pierce through the bowing samurai. (Lucky for him.)

dirt.name: dirt
dirt.description: At first this looks like a cross in the ground, but it's actually something growing in the dirt. It's a little sprout, already rocking three leaves. And it's all thanks to that dirt! When you see this radical, think about how amazing it is that things just, like, grow in the dirt.

thousand.name: thousand
thousand.description: You have a slide supported by a big cross, which makes it so strong it can hold a lot of people at once. How many? A THOUSAND! Imagine a thousand people going down this slide. You can hear a voice trying to count them as they go: "ONE! TWO! THREE HUNDRED! FIVE HUNDRED!" and so on. Finally the last person slides down and the voice calls out: "A THOUSAND!" You think it's over, but just as that group finishes, in come another thousand…

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

evening.name: evening
evening.description: This is the top half of a moon peeking out as it begins to rise. When does that happen? In the evening! Don't confuse this with the moon radical 月. 夕 is for the evening, when the moon's still low enough to be partly covered (by a mountain, for example).

child.name: child
child.description: This is the radical for child. Aww, see how they're wearing a cap and have their little arms stretched out? You can almost hear them saying "pick me up!" A very child-like child.

small.name: small
small.description: You see two fins around a barb, but when you look closer, you realize those fins are actually two tiny fish. Think how small these fins (and fish) would have to be to look so much smaller than an already small barb. Talk about small fry… Yeah, that's why this is the radical for small.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

street.name: street
street.description: Imagine viewing this one from above — looks like a couple of streets, right? Maybe a three-way intersection at the top there? Well, that's why this is the radical for street.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

barb.name: barb
barb.description: This radical is shaped like a barb. Kind of like the barb you'd see on a barbed fishing hook. Imagine that thing getting stuck in your arm or something. Not exactly pleasant. Say out loud, "Oh dang, I got a barb stuck in me!" Alright, relax, it's only a little barb.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

stool.name: stool
stool.description: This looks just like a folding stool, am I right?

drop.name: drop
drop.description: This little radical is a drop, maybe of water or some other liquid. Notice how it's only one drop? Sometimes radicals and kanji will have more than one drop in them, but don't let that throw you off.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

slide.name: slide
slide.description: Close your eyes and imagine you're a kid again. Now open them and... look! It's a slide! Imagine little you sliding down this slide over and over, having a great time. Imagination + emotion makes for good memorization!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

middle.name: middle
middle.description: This is a stick going through the middle of a mouth. What kind of mouth? Mouth of a cylinder, mouth of a box, a human mouth — whatever kind you want to imagine, as long as the stick is going through the middle of it, since this is the radical for "middle."

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

five.name: five
five.description: If you use your imagination, this radical actually looks like the number "5." You just have to remove the vertical line in the bottom left, plus a few extra ends that stick out here and there. Once you do that, you have an easy way to remember that this radical means five.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

lid.name: lid
lid.description: This looks like something you'd put on a kettle or a pot, right? It even has a little handle to make sure you don't burn yourself. This is some kind of lid.

head.name: head
head.description: This is the outline of a head. A really square head, admittedly, but try to imagine it facing to the right. The left side is the back of the head, and the barb-like part on the right shows the curve of the chin. See the head?

heaven.name: heaven
heaven.description: Remember the guy from the big radical? This is him again, but this time he's so big his head is touching heaven. He's looking up and has his arms stretched open toward heaven, and his big hair is poking into those heavenly clouds.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

hand.name: hand
hand.description: This is the hand radical. If you look at the palm of your right hand, the lines in it probably kinda look like the lines in 手. Sure it's not a perfect match, and everyone's got different hands, but basically this radical looks like the lines in your hand.

writing.name: writing
writing.description: This looks like a stool with a lid on it, but it's actually a desk for writing. Do you see it? You can even see the pen sticking up in the middle. Think of all the beautiful writing that's been produced here. Letters, novels, essays — any kind of writing you can think of.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

sun.name: sun
sun.description: Since there are no circles in Japanese characters, sometimes rectangles or squares have to make do. In this case, this big rectangle is the sun. The middle line is a cloud, moving across the sun. Picture it, but don't actually look at the sun and burn your eyes!

moon.name: moon
moon.description: This is the moon radical. See how it looks like a crescent moon with a couple of little clouds floating by? Try not to mix this up with the evening radical 夕 — you can see the whole crescent moon this time, so obviously this should be the radical for moon!

tree.name: tree
tree.description: Do you see the tree in here? It has branches, and then it has the foliage coming down diagonally from it, spreading out like a tree would. That's why this radical is a tree.

water.name: water
water.description: This is the radical for water. It's the water radical because it kind of looks like water coming out of a faucet and splashing everywhere. See how it flows off in different directions too? Very water-like! As water-like as a radical can be, anyway…

fire.name: fire
fire.description: This is the radical for fire. It's got three little flames on top and even a couple logs underneath.

dog.name: dog
dog.description: Remember the big guy who helped you with that big radical? Now you've got his big DOG! You know it's a dog because he's lying down, legs all spread out, with his favorite bone right beside his head.

king.name: king
king.description: This is the king radical. Picture this king sitting on his solid, squarish throne. The top stroke is the high back of the throne. The cross in the middle is his body, with his arms spread out on the arm rests. The bottom stroke is the ground. See him sitting there now, looking all dignified. Talk about a kingly king.

narwhal.name: narwhal
narwhal.description: This radical looks like a narwhal. See the tail at the bottom, and the majestic narwhal horn poking out at the top? It also looks like the katakana character ナ (na), so you can remember it that way too. Na-na-na-narwhal! Just be careful not to confuse this with the treasure radical (メ). The sideways line for that one is at more of a downward angle, like an "X." But the sideways line for narwhal is horizontal (ナ).

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

legs.name: legs
legs.description: This radical already looks like a pair of legs. There's even feet on these legs. That's how you know these are legs and not fins (or something else).

book.name: book
book.description: What's something useful you can get out of a tree? A book! Just cut that book right out of the tree trunk. If you think about it, every tree contains unlimited book potential. It's just a shame they have to be cut down to make the books…

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

stop.name: stop
stop.description: There's a stick and a giant toe stuck in the ground. Imagine walking along and seeing these things in your path. What would you do? Most likely come to a complete stop. You know, because of the toe. I guess a normal stop sign wasn't good enough, so they also put up this big toe to really get people to stop.

rice-paddy.name: rice-paddy
rice-paddy.description: In Japan rice paddies are made up of square fields with irrigation lines going between them. See the four rice paddies with water flowing between and around them? That's why this radical is rice paddy.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

bow.name: bow
bow.description: This radical looks like a fancy composite bow people would use to shoot at targets and definitely not any normal living animals. See how the middle part is being pulled back? Imagine holding this bow and firing it at a target shaped like a giant alligator-crab monster.

door.name: door
door.description: This one looks like a flag, but don't be fooled — it's got a line over the top of it, kind of like a door frame. That makes this a little door. Specifically a swinging door, with the vertical line on the left being its door hinge. Kind of a weird door, it's true, but you can see it, right?

leaf.name: leaf
leaf.description: There's a leaf on top and a stem going down out of it, making this the leaf radical. It's a pretty wide and flat leaf, so if it helps you can imagine it as a lily pad leaf.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

prison.name: prison
prison.description: This looks like an enclosure of sorts — that's why it's a prison. Imagine a tiny prisoner curled up inside, staring off into space, completely convinced they're trapped forever. Ironically this prison is actually wide open. They're just so consumed with self-pity that they haven't realized they've been free to leave all along.

stand.name: stand
stand.description: This looks like someone in a standing position with legs planted firmly on the ground, spreading their arms out in a T-pose to maintain balance. So that makes this the radical for stand.

cow.name: cow
cow.description: This is the cow radical. It kind of looks like the long face of a cow, right? You can think of that top horizontal line as its horns. Seeing it yet? It's got a little tuft of hair on its head, and some kind of accessory on the horn to your left, so this is a very stylish cow.

heart.name: heart
heart.description: Take a good look at this one. It kind of looks like a heart. Not the heart emoji kind, but a real-deal beating heart. Basically, there's a lump in the middle (the heart), and three lines that make up the arteries. Okay, so it's not the most anatomically correct heart, but just look at those floppy arteries going in and out, doing their thing and keeping you alive. What's not to love?

white.name: white
white.description: Add a drop of the sun to something and it will turn it white. The sun is really strong, so even a single drop will burn anything white. Why do you think so many beaches are white? Even the drops of sunblock you use to stop the sun's rays is white. So when you think of sun drops, think white.

eye.name: eye
eye.description: See the iris in the middle of the eyeball? Remember that there are no round things in kanji, so square things take their place. That makes this a pretty realistic, square eye. You just have to squint with your real eyes a bit.

origin.name: origin
origin.description: You have two lines with legs below them. And what is that bit above the legs? Well, it's where you came from — your origin. Yep, between two legs is where you originated, no matter how you look at it.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

private.name: private
private.description: This is a little triangle that's almost completely closed off. When you want to be alone, you hide in a place where you can be in private. That's why this is the private radical. You're closing yourself off to the rest of the world, with just a little peephole.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

father.name: father
father.description: This is the father radical, since you can see a very fatherly face in it — two droopy eyes at the top, a big nose in the middle, and a mustache flaring out at the bottom. Sort of a generic older gentleman father-type face, isn't it?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fur.name: fur
fur.description: Do you remember the hair radical? This is like that, except now the hair is attached to a tail. Animals don't usually have hair though. Instead, they have fur.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

winter.name: winter
winter.description: This is the radical for winter. It looks like a skier going downhill (to the left), with their skis out in front and their poles out behind them. And when do people ski? In winter!

fingers.name: fingers
fingers.description: This looks like a long index finger bent at the bottom, with several other stubby fingers growing out of it on either side. That makes it the fingers radical. A bit creepy, but imagine how easily you could grip something or scratch your back with fingery fingers like these. Pretty nice, right?

spoon.name: spoon
spoon.description: This radical looks like a spoon! Well, a spoon that's been bent a lot by an angry person or a psychic. Go grab a spoon and try to bend it into this shape. Then hold on to it so you'll never forget that this is the spoon radical!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

old.name: old
old.description: Put a cross over the mouth of a container full of all the old things you no longer need. They've all served their purpose but have gotten old, so it's now time to let them rest in peace under the cross. Goodbye, old things!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

triceratops.name: triceratops
triceratops.description: The three horns represent the triceratops, one of the most awesome dinosaurs ever.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

now.name: now
now.description: This is the radical for now. It's a bit tough to see, but it kind of looks like a fancy clock. The 𠆢 is the top part hanging over the clock. The ラ shows a triangular clock face with a little cuckoo on top, peeking out to tell you this means now — right now!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

dry.name: dry
dry.description: This looks like a clothesline pole you would dry clothes on. It's got two very straight horizontal poles, for maximum drying efficiency. If you were gonna dry your clothes, you'd dry them on this thing.

task.name: task
task.description: This looks like a little whiteboard to keep track of your tasks for the day. It's got three little legs on the bottom. And see those blank squares? They're sticky notes you can write your tasks on. Work tasks, school tasks, WaniKani reviews… So what are your tasks for today? Imagine putting them in those little squares.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

canopy.name: canopy
canopy.description: This is a canopy. See the cloth draping down and the little point at the middle holding it up? You could sit under here and enjoy a bright summer day without getting any sun!

towel.name: towel
towel.description: There's something draped over that stick. It's a towel! It's the kind of towel you clean with, so it's sort of old and crusty, which is why it holds its square shape even when it's hanging on the stick like that.

arrow.name: arrow
arrow.description: This is a gun on top of a big person. The gun is big too, obviously. Why is this big guy's gun so big? Because it shoots arrows! Bullets are pretty small, so the guns that shoot them can be any size. But you need a big gun if you want to shoot a big arrow, right? That's why this is the radical for arrow.

world.name: world
world.description: Use your imagination to draw a circle outside this shape. The grids from the radical start to look like longitude and latitude lines on a globe, don't they? That's why this is the world.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

master.name: master
master.description: The king is hovering a drop of water over his head. He's acquired the ability to manipulate water at will, making him the master of all water! If you think about how all life depends on water, that really makes him the master of everything…

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

leader.name: leader
leader.description: This radical technically means person (like 人), but we're going to shift the meaning a little bit. It's kind of like the person radical, but it's taller and skinnier, standing above the others and raising its fist to lead them. That's why this is a leader, not a regular person.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

direction.name: direction
direction.description: This radical looks like a dude running off to the right. He's heading in a very distinct direction. Which direction was it again?

alligator.name: alligator
alligator.description: See the mouth and long, fang-like teeth of this alligator, which is facing to the right? You wouldn't want to get your hand stuck in there, that's for sure.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

stick.name: stick
stick.description: This is a stick, sticking straight up. It even looks like a stick.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

stone.name: stone
stone.description: A leaf growing out of a… mouth?? Look again and see the mouth is actually made of stone. The leaf here was just growing out of a crack on the side. Makes this stone mouth sculpture look kinda elegant, if you ask me. And obviously leaves don't grow on mouths, so whenever you see a leaf on a mouth, remember it's gotta be stone.

ceremony.name: ceremony
ceremony.description: This radical looks like a person with a long dress running behind them. That's because they're all dressed up to go to a ceremony. You usually have to wear a long dress (or something formal) if you're going to a big ceremony, right? So when you see this radical, think of all the different ceremonies you might have to go to in your life and what you'll need to wear.

horns.name: horns
horns.description: Can you see the horns? You could put these horns on anything. Donkeys, people, monkeys, and so on. That's exactly what we'll be doing when we learn kanji that have horns too.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

forehead.name: forehead
forehead.description: Sure, this forehead is a bit square, but that's because it's the top part of the head (冂) radical. This is just the forehead part of that! You can even imagine this as the forehead of Frankenstein's monster, since his forehead was pretty square too.

branch.name: branch
branch.description: There's a cross branching off of this stool. Since it's branching out, we'll just call this the branch radical. I guess the tree this stool was made of is coming back to life in the form of a branch.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

plate.name: plate
plate.description: This is a few plates drying on a rack. They're stacked together in a line. Imagine reaching out and grabbing one of those plates for yourself. And you only need one, so let's call this radical plate.

umbrella.name: umbrella
umbrella.description: This radical looks like an umbrella, specifically just the handle part. A good old curvy umbrella handle, so you can hang that umbrella on your arm when it stops raining.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

spirit.name: spirit
spirit.description: This radical looks like some sort of spirit. It faces to the left with a big hood over its head, and creepy arms slanting downward at the sides. Can you picture it?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

roof.name: roof
roof.description: This is a roof ー a flat roof with a little chimney sticking out. How cute!

gun.name: gun
gun.description: This looks a lot like a gun pointing to the right. You have a handle on the left, and the barrel pointing to the right. Imagine it hanging in a case on the wall in a museum.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

energy.name: energy
energy.description: This is the radical for energy. If you imagine a train under this radical, then the lines would be steam bursting out of it, flowing back from the front of the train (on the left) as it goes chugging along. Steam was the best way to generate energy back in the day, you know? That's why this is the radical for energy.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

thread.name: thread
thread.description: In some poop you find something small. You lean in, carefully pull it out, and realize it's a thread. What's that doing there? Well, it came from a cat, and cats love to eat thread when you aren't looking. And floss and hoodie strings and mouse tails… But this time, just thread.

insect.name: insect
insect.description: This looks like the middle radical on top of a sideways leaf. What can you often find sitting on the middle of a leaf? An insect. Insects looove sitting right in the middle of leaves. That's why this is the insect radical.

bamboo.name: bamboo
bamboo.description: See how tall the stalks are with little bushy things at the top? It kind of looks like bamboo, if you use your imagination.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

treasure.name: treasure
treasure.description: This radical is treasure because "X marks the spot," as they say. Doesn't it look like the X on a pirate treasure map? You want to go find that treasure now, right?!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

measurement.name: measurement
measurement.description: Have you ever used one of those triangular rulers? This looks just like one of those, because this is the measurement radical. Just think of all the angles you could measure with this thing!

see.name: see
see.description: This eye has legs, since you have to let your eyes run over something in order to see it. That's why those eyes need legs — to see. They're wandering eyes, they want to see the world!!

flowers.name: flowers
flowers.description: See the little things sticking out of the ground? Those little things are pretty little flowers.

ear.name: ear
ear.description: This is the ear radical, since it looks like an ear. As much as a radical can, anyway. Those two middle lines are the ridges that guide the sound into your ear, and the lines that stick out to the right are where the ear attaches to your head.

foot.name: foot
foot.description: This is the foot radical. See the person doing a flying dragon kick to the right? What do they hit the target with? Obviously their foot. Imagine that foot coming straight at your face. At that point, your only hope is that it's clean. Nobody wants a foot to the face, especially not a filthy foot.

shellfish.name: shellfish
shellfish.description: This radical looks like a shellfish. What looks like the eye radical is its long spiral shell. The bottom part that looks like fins is its little legs poking out, helping the shellfish move around.

red.name: red
red.description: A pile of dirt fell on you and now your legs and (scuba) fins are sticking out from under it, turning red. You know, because you've been stuck in the sun so long. Everything's all sunburned and red. Can scuba fins get a sunburn? Yeah, when it's red hot outside. That's why this means "red."

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

not.name: not
not.description: This looks like a regular arrow, but it's not. Let's call it the "not arrow." It can not move forward, because it's been stopped by some kind of line above it. Every arrow wants to fly, but this one is not going anywhere. Look at it, being all negated like that. Well, that's just what this radical does. It nots things.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

car.name: car
car.description: Doesn't this look like a top-down view of the frame of a car? There are the axles at the top and bottom, and the body in the middle between them. That's why this is the car radical.

samurai.name: samurai
samurai.description: The samurai radical looks a lot like the dirt radical. The difference is this one has a longer top horizontal line, which is for those broad warrior shoulders. The bottom line is also shorter, since it's the little cushion the samurai is sitting on. Samurai didn't just fight, you know. They sat on cushions and wrote poems and stuff too. This is a very sensitive samurai.

meet.name: meet
meet.description: Under the hat there are two people meeting in private. Look at them huddled up in there. They must have a reason to want to meet in such a private place, but that's not for you to judge. Maybe next time you have to meet with someone you'll hide under a hat with them too.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

mustache.name: mustache
mustache.description: See the mustache going around the mouth? Man, I wish I could grow a sweet mustache like this. (Sure, the mustache looks a lot like the head radical here, but just imagine it as a mustache this time!)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

window.name: window
window.description: See the criss-cross in the almost-square shape? That's the frames of a big window you can look out of. It's not perfectly shaped like a rice field, it's more artistic. That's why this is a window and 田 is not.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

pi.name: pi
pi.description: Obviously, this looks just like the good ol' 3.14 something-something all people are supposed to learn about. It's the math symbol that has to do with circles... pi!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

wolverine.name: wolverine
wolverine.description: You know Wolverine from the X-Men? He has three claws that come out of his hand whenever he needs to fight, not at all unlike the radical you see here.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

rice.name: rice
rice.description: This is the radical for rice. Specifically raw rice, like the grain that grows out of the ground when rice is raw and fresh. When rice is particularly ripe, it'll kind of look like this at the top of the stalks. See the long leaves and the little rice tufts at the top? That's why this radical is "rice."

feathers.name: feathers
feathers.description: This radical already looks like a pair of rectangular feathers, right? I guess those short lines in each feather are like the filaments of the feathers.

tsunami.name: tsunami
tsunami.description: A single drop (丶) is just water. But as soon as there are three, rising higher and higher, you know what's coming. A tsunami! Think of those drops as wave crests. Three whole tsunami waves, stacking up? That's a big tsunami.

beggar.name: beggar
beggar.description: Doesn't this look like someone on their knees, holding their clasped hands out and begging for something (forgiveness, their life, a promotion, etc.)? That's why this radical is known as the beggar.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

meat.name: meat
meat.description: This is the radical for meat. What's meat-like about this? Think of a nice square slab of meat with some beautiful 人-shaped marbling lines in it. Seeing it now? Mmm, meat.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

self.name: self
self.description: Remember the eye radical? Imagine that's your eye now, and there's a drop of paint falling into it, making your eye color unique. Now it's what makes you you. It's what makes you your self.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

coffin.name: coffin
coffin.description: See the slide going into the dirt? What appears to be a slide here is actually a coffin, sliding into the dirt to be buried.

key.name: key
key.description: This is a very oddly shaped key, but it's a key nonetheless.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

lip-ring.name: lip-ring
lip-ring.description: This looks like something hooked around the corner of a mouth — it's a lip ring! Use your imagination, because it's either a giant lip ring or a very small mouth. It's angular because kanji prefer straight lines, but try to picture it as the kind of studded, angular lip ring your trendy friend wore in high school.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

ice.name: ice
ice.description: Remember the radical for drop (of liquid)? Looks like it's shattered into two pieces here, which means it must be ice now. You know, since a drop could only break in two after turning into ice.

west.name: west
west.description: You have the legs of a bird with wings outspread, landing in the mouth of her nest to rest. And when does she go back to rest? When the sun sets in the west! Imagine all the birds flying home as the sun sets. That's why this radical means west.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

color.name: color
color.description: In prison, dreaming in bed is the only way to see color. Picture a dark, dank cell with four gray walls. Now imagine the colorful dreamscapes your bed would take you to every night. What a contrast! That's why a prison bed = color.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

go.name: go
go.description: You have the radicals for loiter, two, and barb. Someone who uses two barbs on you while you're loitering really wants you to GO!! GO on, stop loitering outside of my convenience store, ya dang kids!

flag.name: flag
flag.description: See the flag in this radical? There's a pole with a flag on the end of it, blowing in the wind. Basically, this radical looks just like a flag.

hair.name: hair
hair.description: These three streaks look like the flowing locks of someone's hair. Pretty easy to see and remember that this is the hair radical, right?

lantern.name: lantern
lantern.description: Think of an old-style lantern. It's in a box-shaped holder lined with rice paper and sits on four legs (you can only see two from this angle though). You can imagine people using these before electricity, right? That's why this radical is a lantern.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

hat.name: hat
hat.description: Do you know those rice paddy hats? The ones shaped just like this radical? That's why this radical is a hat.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

run.name: run
run.description: See the person running (to the right)? That's why this is the radical for run. Just don't confuse this with foot (足). The head in this one has streaks around it because the person is running so fast.

say.name: say
say.description: There are a bunch of lines coming out of a mouth. Think of them as speech lines in a little speech bubble. Those lines represent the things you say.

scooter.name: scooter
scooter.description: This radical is a scooter. See the handlebars and the little light? And there's a platform at the bottom you can stand or sit on (if it's one of those nice motorized scooters).

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

village.name: village
village.description: A rice paddy and some dirt — dirt roads, in fact, because this is a village. Small villages like this are mostly rice paddies and dirt (roads), you know? You could even think of this as a bird's-eye view of a village.

axe.name: axe
axe.description: This is the radical for axe, since it looks like one. The two horizontal strokes are the head, the long left line is the whole edge of its blade, and the vertical line in the middle is its short handle. Kind of a strange axe, I admit, but an axe is an axe.

viking.name: viking
viking.description: On the top part of the head (冂) radical, there is a horn radical with one extra spike in the middle. This is a horned helmet with one extra spike. Who were famous for having horned helmets like that? The Vikings! That's why this radical is called Viking.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

lack.name: lack
lack.description: A (modified) prison radical containing a person radical. What does a person experience in prison? Lack. Prison is all about lack — lack of freedom, lack of the comforts of home, lack of privacy, lack of conversation. Yep, the life of a person in prison is defined by lack.

gold.name: gold
gold.description: Underneath that hat are two bars of gold, but under those there are some spikes. This is some kind of trap for people who try to take your gold. And why else would you make a trap? Everyone wants to protect their gold.

blue.name: blue
blue.description: You have life on top of the moon (ignore that life is missing its little slide on the left side). What would life on the moon look like? Well, first of all, they have no air, so they probably look pretty blue from asphyxiation. When you meet them, it turns out you're right. They're a civilization of blue people. Imagine them all living up there, being blue and Smurfy.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

sheep.name: sheep
sheep.description: This is a very fluffy sheep. See the two horns poking up and all its wool poking out to the side? It's so fluffy that you can hardly see its tiny little legs. What a cute sheep!

yakuza.name: yakuza
yakuza.description: This radical looks like a yakuza. See him facing left, with that threatening jawline and big pompadour hairstyle? Maybe imagine a couple scars on his face too. You know, real yakuza-like.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

city.name: city
city.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means city.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

blood.name: blood
blood.description: You see a little drop of something on your plate. Oh no… it's BLOOD!!! Yep, you're bleeding. Now you have to wash that plate again — just as soon as the bleeding stops. Don't worry, your platelets should stop the bleeding soon. Haha, get it? PLATE-lets? Like blood platelets? Japanese blood jokes.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

jet.name: jet
jet.description: This is like one of those jets that have the little nose-wings going on too. Imagine you're looking at the jet from the top down, this is what it would look like. It's also why this is the jet radical.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

life.name: life
life.description: Remember the sprout from the dirt radical? This looks like that, but wait a minute… there's some extra growth now! This thing is so full of life! It's even got a big, slide-shaped leaf growing on the top left. That's why it's the life radical.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

lion.name: lion
lion.description: This L-shaped radical is the lion radical, because "L" is for "Lion." It also looks like a lion's mouth being opened really, really wide. Imagine a lion opening its mouth to yawn. It looks like this radical!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

long.name: long
long.description: When a stick figure has hair that drags on the ground and gets kicked all the time, you know that hair's gotta be long. Why is it so looooong?!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

rain.name: rain
rain.description: The towel is getting soaked with drops of liquid. That's because it's raining. Get your towels and other clothes inside before they get soaked through from this rain!

twenty.name: twenty
twenty.description: You have 十 plus 十 (never mind that the left one is kinda curvy)... What do you get when you add these two kanji together? You get twenty.

poop.name: poop
poop.description: For better or worse, this looks enough like the poop emoji (💩) to make this the poop radical. Try to remember it for its distinct outline. If it helps you can also note the private radical on the bottom of this one, since you should probably be in private when you poop…

box.name: box
box.description: This box is open. There could be any number of things inside, but for now all you need to know is the meaning of this radical, which is box.

boil.name: boil
boil.description: This radical looks like the fire from the burner of a stove. That's because this is the boil radical. You need to boil a bunch of water over some flames.

tongue.name: tongue
tongue.description: This is the tongue radical. See that sturdy slide holding a thousand people above a mouth? An open mouth can't support that much weight, but a strong tongue can — it's the muscle of the mouth, after all.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

loiter.name: loiter
loiter.description: The leader (ｲ) radical has a little something extra on top of him — he's got a casual cap on because it's his day off. And what does he like to do on his day off? He likes to loiter. Look at him just loitering around. What a loiterer.

ladle.name: ladle
ladle.description: There's a ladle scooping up two drops of liquid. See the handle and the scoop part of the ladle? Imagine ladling some water into your mouth. Mmm.

grain.name: grain
grain.description: This is a tree with a drooping slide shape on top. What causes the top of plants to sag? The grains. Imagine seeing how the heads of wheat or rice droop when they're ready for harvest. It will look like this radical. Look at all that ripe grain!

clan.name: clan
clan.description: Under a cliff, you spot a cross, slightly tilted. The cross is there to mark a famous clan that once ruled the lands below the cliff. It's a pretty unique cross, so whenever you see it there under the cliff, you'll know it honors the clan who lived there.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

angel.name: angel
angel.description: First of all, there's a cross making up the middle, which automatically makes this a religion-based radical. That cross is the frame of someone's body and the things coming off of it are wings. This is an angel.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fortune.name: fortune
fortune.description: Someone has a toe in their mouth. They are a fortune teller telling your fortune. But for her to do it, she has to take one of your toes and taste it. This better be a good fortune for you to let someone put your foot in their mouth.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

eat.name: eat
eat.description: You put on your hat and go outside to kick something white. It's a big white goose and you killed it with your kick. That's because you're going to cook and eat it. Yum!

neck.name: neck
neck.description: This is the radical for neck. Look, you can see all the vertebrae stacked up, and the little ligaments that connect it to the base of the skull! That's why this is the radical for neck.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

husband.name: husband
husband.description: The radicals ground and big make up this radical. There's a big guy on the ground... who is that big guy? Embarrassingly, it's your husband. Feel the shame that he got knocked down on the ground. By what? Who? How? (It doesn't matter.)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

pig.name: pig
pig.description: Here we have the ground radical, with something underneath it. What likes lying in the ground, covered in mud? A pig! Use your imagination and see if you can find the head near the top, the piggy tail at the bottom right, and the little piglets on the left, nestling up to this mama pig.

knife.name: knife
knife.description: This is a knife. See the sharp edge on the left and the tapered tip on top? And there's a little handle at the bottom for the... well, the handle. Be careful you don't cut yourself on this knife!

mama.name: mama
mama.description: This radical is just like the katakana character "ma" (マ). So let's keep things simple and make the radical mama. It even looks like a mama leaning over and gently tapping her baby to help them fall asleep.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

temple.name: temple
temple.description: This radical looks kind of like a temple, don't you think? With all the layers and stuff? Just picture the top part as a roof, and the drop-looking bit at the bottom as a giant temple bell, with a monk standing beside it and ringing it with a large wooden beam. You see this kinda thing every day at a temple.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

teacher.name: teacher
teacher.description: Who's in the coffin next to that child? It's his teacher. Teachers are pretty much dead around 3 p.m. every day, and it's the kids in their class who wear them down. It ain't easy being a teacher, you know.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

oneself.name: oneself
oneself.description: This kinda looks like a head with an arm under it pointing up at itself. That's why this radical means oneself. One points to oneself when one wants to… indicate oneself…

top-hat.name: top-hat
top-hat.description: This radical looks just like a top hat. That's why it's top hat!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

circumference.name: circumference
circumference.description: You take your head and pack clay dirt around it to just above your mouth. You need the circumference of your head for your helmet maker, and sending them a clay dirt mold of your head will just do the trick. That way they'll know your head's exact circumference!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fish.name: fish
fish.description: This is a fish. Kind of a funny-looking fish, with a weird head on top, a squat scaly body, and a tail fanning out underneath it. See the fish? Yeah, you see it.

bird.name: bird
bird.description: See the cool wing on the bottom? See the head with the neat feather coming out of the top? This is a bird. Tweet tweet!

black.name: black
black.description: If you boil a village, everything will turn black. Just imagine all those roads melting and coating everything in black tar. Picture it! It looks like Pompeii. (Oof, too soon?)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

cage.name: cage
cage.description: This radical is a cage because it's covered on all sides but one (the one open side is where the cage door or gate is). There's nothing inside this cage because there's no gate, and whatever was inside has gotten out. I hope it wasn't rabid...

yellow.name: yellow
yellow.description: Flowers that grow out of the ground of a rice paddy after you bury fins there will grow yellow. Why? Because yellow is the color of a fish's soul, so planting flowers over their fins will turn them into a nice, pretty yellow.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

boat.name: boat
boat.description: This looks like a boat coming toward you in the water. See the sail billowing on top and the reflection on the water? Yep, that's a boat. The boatiest boat that ever boated.

cape.name: cape
cape.description: This is a cape, like the kind a superhero would wear. Use your imagination to picture a hero facing left, with a cape tied around their neck and hanging down their back. With that cape on, they're ready to save the world!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

hook.name: hook
hook.description: This looks like a hook, like the kind a pirate would wear. The flat line shows where the hand was cut off, and the hook is right above, ready to take its place. Yarrrr.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

weapon.name: weapon
weapon.description: Stick a table on top of a stool and you end up with a very dangerous weapon. Imagine hitting someone with this thing! Or, even better, imagine making someone sit on it. They'd be crushed mentally and emotionally and whatnot. Now that's a scary weapon.

hole.name: hole
hole.description: You have a roof on top of what looks like a volcano. What's the roof covering? It's definitely covering the hole at the top. I guess someone thought if they built a roof on the hole, none of the lava could come out. But isn't there a hole in the chimney? Guess their theory had some holes in it… Oh no, now lava is coming through the hole. RUN!!! RUN!!!!!!!!

horse.name: horse
horse.description: This radical looks like a horse. See the long, thick neck with the mane flowing to the right? What might look like a wing is actually the horse's four legs and its tail hanging down.

easy.name: easy
easy.description: Flying to the sun with wings is easy! Or so Icarus thinks. Someone should probably tell him…

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

suit.name: suit
suit.description: This is the radical for suit, like when something suits you. You find a hat on the ground, and when you pick it up, there's a mouth underneath. "It suits you! It suits you!" the mouth says. You try the hat on, and yup — it really suits! It's weird getting fashion advice from a random mouth, but hey, it wasn't wrong.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

net.name: net
net.description: This is a net, spread out over the water. Imagine all the fish you can catch in this net!

sickle.name: sickle
sickle.description: Most of this radical is just the rice radical, but the top part is something coming to cut the rice from its stalk. That, my friend, is the sickle. See how the rice is still there? That's because it's in motion. The sickle is literally doing the cutting RIGHT NOW! (And don't confuse this with the grain 禾 radical!)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

gate.name: gate
gate.description: This radical already looks like a gate, right? A high gate with pillars on either side and two small gate doors, way up at the top for some reason. Worst gate ever in terms of security, but definitely still a gate.

cloud.name: cloud
cloud.description: This is the radical for cloud. See how the cloud is puffy on the bottom but has two lines up top? That's because a plane flew through it.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

history.name: history
history.description: This is the history radical. You can see a textbook and a couple crossed swords. Because that's basically how history works — fight battles, record the results, and do it all over again. History!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

brush.name: brush
brush.description: This radical kind of looks like a brush. The top part is a hand holding the brush handle, and the bottom part is the bristly tip.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

machine.name: machine
machine.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means machine.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

cleat.name: cleat
cleat.description: There's the bottom of a shoe with spikes coming out of it. Only one kind of shoe is like this, and that's a cleat. You know, like those studded shoes athletes wear. Soccer cleats, baseball cleats, running cleats… You knew they were called cleats, right?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

correct.name: correct
correct.description: You're lost, but you've come across a signpost that says "correct" in all directions. "Correct," "correct," "correct," it says. No matter which way you go, it's the correct way! What a relief!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

spring.name: spring
spring.description: This is the spring radical, because it looks like a new plant shoot has pushed its way up through layers of dirt, snow, and ice to announce the coming of spring. Spring is the season (and the radical) full of new, growing life!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

stamp.name: stamp
stamp.description: This is the stamp radical — the inking kind, not the postage kind. Why? Because it looks like a stamp stick! The top parts are thicker on one side so you can get a good grip. It even looks like the letter P — which just happens to be the last letter in "stamp"!

eternity.name: eternity
eternity.description: A drop of water will last for an eternity. Especially if you drop it into an endless stream that never stops flowing. You can see these streams converging from all directions here, flowing for eternity.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

wing.name: wing
wing.description: See the wing? It's kind of like a bat wing with webbing. Either way, it's just a wing.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

kick.name: kick
kick.description: This looks like a stick-figure foot on the left kicking at something, which makes it the radical for kick. See the shin and foot swinging up for the kick? You can think of that part on the right as the edge of a ball that's about to be kicked.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

yurt.name: yurt
yurt.description: There's a canopy with a grid-like frame near the top. It's a yurt with a window! Yurts are those dome-shaped huts nomads use. Once you've set up a canopy with a window frame, you've basically built one. Imagine gazing out that window, kicking back in your yurt. Yurt sweet yurt!

jammed-in.name: jammed-in
jammed-in.description: This radical looks like a hat with some legs under it. If all you can see is legs, the person wearing the hat must really be jammed in there. Have you ever tried to cram yourself into a hat so hard it covers your body? Yeah, didn't think so. But imagine how jammed in to that hat you'd be if you did.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

tent.name: tent
tent.description: Kind of a strange tent, I'll admit, but one that will protect you from things trying to get in (that's why it has all the spikes). It's the best you can do, considering you're in a cloth tent.

clown.name: clown
clown.description: A clown face, with the funny hat at the top, the silly eyes in the middle, and the big laughing mouth at the bottom. This thing is pretty cute, unlike a lot of clowns…

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

building.name: building
building.description: This radical looks like a curvy letter B. What does B stand for? Building! This radical often shows up in kanji related to gatherings or to places where people come together… like buildings.

helicopter.name: helicopter
helicopter.description: This radical looks like a helicopter coming straight at you, doesn't it? You can't see the tail part because it's behind the bigger front cab of the helicopter. But you can see the front windows and the big spinning blade on top.

heavy.name: heavy
heavy.description: This is the heavy radical. See the square fist in the center, grasping the handle of a dumbbell that has two heavy plates on the top and bottom? The radical just looks like it would be heavy, you know?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

gladiator.name: gladiator
gladiator.description: Someone shoved horns onto your husband and said he has to fight for them now. They turned him into a gladiator! Now your husband has to fight for their amusement. Are you not entertained?!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

bundle.name: bundle
bundle.description: This looks like a tree with something tied around its middle, holding it tight. If you untied that middle part you'd realize it's actually a bunch of little trees (aka twigs and sticks) tied in a bundle!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

anti.name: anti
anti.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means anti.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

kiss.name: kiss
kiss.description: In winter, your mouth longs for a kiss. You know, because your lips get cold and you want someone to warm them up. How do you think that whole mistletoe thing got started? Cold winter mouths, longing for a kiss. So just imagine the top part of this radical as some winter mistletoe (or holly leaves, since that's probably easier), and the bottom part as a kissy mouth.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

spikes.name: spikes
spikes.description: On the ground there are a bunch of spikes coming out. You wouldn't want to step on those now, would you?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

chinese.name: chinese
chinese.description: This is the Chinese radical. Why? Because it looks like a traditional Chinese pagoda! One of those towers with a bunch of tiered roofs, you know? Sure, lots of Asian countries have pagodas, but let's be honest — the Chinese probably came up with them first.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

sell.name: sell
sell.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means sell.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

music.name: music
music.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means music! 曲 even looks like a musical score with two notes in the middle. Huh? Real sheet music has more lines, you say? Hey, it's a radical — we're saving ink, not performing Beethoven here.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

landslide.name: landslide
landslide.description: If you kick a cliff twice you'll start a landslide! Oh no!

fault.name: fault
fault.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means fault.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

beans.name: beans
beans.description: This radical has a bean (the square in the middle) between the ground and some roots. That bean is going to turn into a plant someday. What will that plant produce? Great big beans, like the ones from Jack and the Beanstalk.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

geoduck.name: geoduck
geoduck.description: A leaf-shaped protrusion coming out of a shellfish. What is it? The mysterious geoduck! The geoduck is a giant clam so big that most of it grows outside its shell. If you've never seen a geoduck, look it up now. You'll never be able to unsee it.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

tombstone.name: tombstone
tombstone.description: There is a stool sticking out of the dirt. You walk up and realize it has writing on it. It's actually a tombstone. You can sit on it and remember the people who are below the dirt now.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

dynamite.name: dynamite
dynamite.description: Doesn't this radical look like one of those old-fashioned dynamite plunger things that gets pushed to set the fuse? That makes this dynamite!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

alcohol.name: alcohol
alcohol.description: This looks like the west radical with a little something extra under it… And if you ask for "a little something extra" in the Wild West, you're going to get a big ol' decanter of bourbon, moonshine, or some other kind of very strong alcohol — which happens to be exactly what this radical looks like! That extra line at the bottom is just the little bit of alcohol still left in the bottle.

cliff.name: cliff
cliff.description: See the cliff? It's facing to the left. What do you think this cliff is jutting out over? Maybe an ocean, or a valley? Cliffs love to jut out over things like that.

drunkard.name: drunkard
drunkard.description: This is a ceremony radical with a slide radical. Why would a ceremony need a slide? For drunkards, of course! People love to drink at ceremonies, and when they've had too much, those drunkards like to go down slides. If your ceremony has a slide, the drunkards will be happy.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

blackjack.name: blackjack
blackjack.description: You have the radical twenty and the kanji one. Put those together and you get 21. What's another name for 21? That's blackjack.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

sick.name: sick
sick.description: This is the canopy radical with ice running down the back. If you hang out in a cold canopy like this you'll definitely get sick. Imagine being sick and alone inside a cold canopy. Brrrr, oh no... ACHOO!

turkey.name: turkey
turkey.description: This is the turkey radical. It kinda looks like a turkey facing to the left, with big tail feathers fanning out behind it. See it? You can practically hear this thing gobble!

orders.name: orders
orders.description: This radical means orders, because it looks like Napoleon with his big bicorne hat, giving orders. You move, or his orders move you.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

few.name: few
few.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means few.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

trash.name: trash
trash.description: There's a lid on top of something you're trying to keep private. It's your trash. You wouldn't want people going through your private trash, which is why we put lids on our trash cans. It's also illegal to look through someone's trash, so make sure you never do that.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

skin.name: skin
skin.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means skin.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

bear.name: bear
bear.description: This is the radical for bear, because it looks like the letter B — B for bear! You know this one is "bear" and not "building" because it's all boxy, like a stocky, chunky bear who's all fat and ready to hibernate.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

humble.name: humble
humble.description: This radical is the same as the kanji you learned earlier, but the meaning is a little different. It's not just for saying things humbly, it's for being humble in general. When you see this radical, think about being humble.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

escalator.name: escalator
escalator.description: Remember the stairs (乃) radical? This is a lot like that, but it has another stroke to motorize it. The extra bit makes the stairs move by themselves — that's why this is an escalator!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

older-brother.name: older-brother
older-brother.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means older brother.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

simple.name: simple
simple.description: This the radical for simple. Why? Because it looks like a simple little stick figure drawing! Just a big head on top with a few hairs, and a stick figure body. What could be more simple?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

root.name: root
root.description: Imagine a root vegetable, like a turnip or something. See the twisted bulb at the top, with long, stringy root tendrils dangling under it? That's why this is the root radical.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

yoga.name: yoga
yoga.description: This looks like someone sitting with their back straight and a leg stretched out behind them. What kind of pose is this? Probably the kind you do in yoga. Sit on a yoga mat and make your body look like this radical. Nice, you're really doing yoga now.

excuse.name: excuse
excuse.description: This radical consists of some legs carrying the sun out of prison. Those legs are yours. When the police stop you, though, what's your excuse? Come up with one and say it out loud, starting with, "My excuse is..."

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

tofu.name: tofu
tofu.description: See the T and F that make up this radical? Those letters stand for tofu, our favorite bean curd blocks.

jackhammer.name: jackhammer
jackhammer.description: Do you see the handles on the top and the barb on the bottom? It's a jackhammer. How can you tell? The two diagonal lines on the side show the motion of the jackhammer going up and down really fast, doing what jackhammers do. BBBHHRZHHHHRHHRZHZHHZH JUJUJUJUJU.

asia.name: asia
asia.description: This looks like the helicopter radical with something extra under it. It's carrying some kind of cargo. Where's it headed? To Asia. You only know it's going to Asia because the cargo has a big TO ASIA label on it. (Go ahead and imagine it now.)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

request.name: request
request.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means request.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

compare.name: compare
compare.description: This radical is made up of two slightly different versions of the spoon radical. Look closely at these two spoons. Compare their differences, like the different angles in the bends of the spoons. It's like one of those "spot the difference" games where you compare two similar pictures. But this time it's with spoons.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

together.name: together
together.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means together. And see how it kind of looks like two people happily sitting together arm-in-arm? These two clearly enjoy doing stuff together. They're together so much they're practically joined at the hip.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

hundred.name: hundred
hundred.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means hundred.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

east.name: east
east.description: Behind that tree the sun is rising in the east. The sun always rises in the east, so if you head that way, past the tree, you'll be going east too.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

younger-brother.name: younger-brother
younger-brother.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means younger brother.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

sound.name: sound
sound.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means sound. You can even see someone standing at a kind of square table here. It's a DJ at the turntables! Imagine them spinning records and scratching out all kinds of cool sounds.

idea.name: idea
idea.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means idea.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

table.name: table
table.description: What happens when you put a flat surface on top of a pair of legs? You get a table.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

womb.name: womb
womb.description: Legs appear between two lumps of dirt. This is the earth's version of a womb. Imagine a tiny baby growing under the ground and then sprouting out, legs first, into the world. Imagine being inside this dirt womb. It's warm and damp. You feel safe.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fruit.name: fruit
fruit.description: There's a rice paddy on top of a tree… Figuratively, because trees don't bear rice, they bear fruit! Use your imagination, and those four little boxes on top of the tree kind of look like big, ripe fruits — just waiting to be picked.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

bookshelf.name: bookshelf
bookshelf.description: This looks like a bunch of books on a shelf. It's a bookshelf!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

original.name: original
original.description: See that cliff with the white and small bits in it? That's an original cliff — we call it that because the white and small bits are actually the bones and fossils of our planet's original inhabitants, buried in the cliffside. (And this one's got a massive sign that says "ORIGINAL," because everything's gotta be a tourist spot these days… sigh.)

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

good.name: good
good.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means good.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

elephant.name: elephant
elephant.description: This is an elephant — or at least, a wannabe elephant. Look closely. It's actually a family of pigs, and the mama is wearing an elephant mask on her head for some reason. See the big ears and trunk on top? She wants to be an elephant so badly that she dressed up like one.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

body.name: body
body.description: The radical is the same as the kanji, so it means body.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

well.name: well
well.description: This is the radical for well — the kind you use to draw up water. Picture an old-timey stone well with wooden posts holding up a crank for reeling up your bucket! Pretty well built, huh?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

circle.name: circle
circle.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means circle.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

part.name: part
part.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means part.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

man.name: man
man.description: A rice paddy needs power, and that power comes from a man. Picture a rice paddy full of thick, wet mud. It's no easy field to work in, but this powerful man will bend over to rake and hoe tirelessly to make sure it thrives. What a man!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

soul.name: soul
soul.description: You spot a wispy shape flanked by ethereal orbs of flame. Don't be scared — this is just a friendly little soul on its way to get reincarnated into a new body.

animal.name: animal
animal.description: This radical swings like a monkey, howls like a wolf, and pounces like a leopard. You can almost hear the jungle chorus… This is the radical for animal!

pope.name: pope
pope.description: This is the pope radical. Why? Because it looks like a pope hat! This one's got a big old cross on top, and it can only be worn by the pope.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

face.name: face
face.description: You have a leaf, a mouth, and an eye. Those are all things you'll find on a face! Wait, you don't have a leaf growing out of your face? Huh... well just imagine you have a normal human face with a leaf on there somewhere, ya weirdo.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

center.name: center
center.description: Remember the guy from the big radical? Here he's bending his arms at the elbow to fasten some kind of belt, right across the center of his body. It's one of those belts that just goes across your waist, which is actually perfect for such a big guy, since he really needs to secure his center (of gravity).

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

valley.name: valley
valley.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means valley.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

squid.name: squid
squid.description: See the squid shape here? There's its pointy head, its eyes, and its tentacles (two, anyway). Pretty squid-like if you ask me!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

snake.name: snake
snake.description: This is the snake radical. See the head at the top with its long skinny body stretching down from it?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

nothing.name: nothing
nothing.description: There are a bunch of cages boiling. But you look inside the cages and there... there's nothing. You boiled whatever was in there down to nothing!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

drum.name: drum
drum.description: This radical is made up of samurai and beans. Samurai used to put beans in drums so they could tap and shake them to make noise. Can you imagine shaking and tapping this drum radical?

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

walk.name: walk
walk.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means walk.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

tiger.name: tiger
tiger.description: You lost seven toes on a cliff, but you consider yourself lucky considering you got them taken off by the cliff tiger that lives in the area. Imagine a tiger taking your seven toes as you hang off the edge of a cliff, if it helps. Exciting! (And also painful!)

festival.name: festival
festival.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means festival.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

bar.name: bar
bar.description: It's the drunkard from before, but this time she's leaning on some kind of horizontal surface. Knowing her, it's probably the counter of a bar. Hopefully she's not causing too much trouble for the bar staff with her drunken antics.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

long-ago.name: long-ago
long-ago.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means long ago.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

previous.name: previous
previous.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means previous.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

treasure-chest.name: treasure-chest
treasure-chest.description: There's a treasure radical inside this box. What holds treasures? A treasure chest.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

paragraph.name: paragraph
paragraph.description: In prison, your mouth is not allowed to talk freely. Get a piece of paper to use in place of your mouth and start writing your thoughts out in paragraph form!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

attach.name: attach
attach.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means attach.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

good-luck.name: good-luck
good-luck.description: This is the samurai radical on top of the mouth radical. If you get to hear what a samurai says, you'll have really good luck. Samurai are pretty hard to find nowadays, which is probably why my luck has been so bad...

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

right.name: right
right.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means right.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

director.name: director
director.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means director.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

reason.name: reason
reason.description: This radical is the same as the kanji. It means reason.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

explosion.name: explosion
explosion.description: See the energy bursting outward? That's an explosion. Boom!

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

fly.name: fly
fly.description: This radical is pretty complicated, but not much looks like this radical, so you're in luck. This looks like a giant flying fortress of some kind. See the propellers on the back as it moves to the left? Just imagine something like this flying through the air. That's why this radical is fly, because it is flying.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

grass.name: grass
grass.description: Don't confuse this with triceratops. Grass is closer together, and a little bushier, like this. It's also more random and squished down in parts. If you see a bush of lines like this, it's probably going to be grass.

vietnamese.note: WaniKani-specific radical (no traditional bộ thủ equivalent)

mole.name: mole
mole.description: There's trash on some dirt that seems to be... moving? If you lift up that trash you'll find a mole in the dirt! Moles like to dig around underground and look for stuff to grab (like trash). They're the raccoons of the ground.
